title: Use Oban.insert/2 in an Ecto.Multi (transactional enqueue)
prompt: Refactor `test_fixtures/two_call_enqueue_context.ex` so that `MyApp.Accounts.register_user/1` inserts the user and enqueues `MyApp.Workers.WelcomeEmailWorker` inside a single `Ecto.Multi` transaction. Use `Oban.insert/3` with a multi key of `:welcome_email` and pass a function that receives the Multi context and builds the job from `user.id`. Use string-keyed args. Return `{:ok, user}` on success or `{:error, changeset}` on user-insert failure. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Well-documented pattern; the two-call bug is obvious once stated and the fix is a single Multi chain.
research_citation: Oban module HexDocs — 'Enqueue a job along with other database changes, ensuring that everything is committed or rolled back atomically.'
